description: Generalize GitHub issue templates so they describe harness runtime context instead of a stale application stack.

userStories.title: Bug template asks for harness context
userStories.description: As a harness maintainer, I want bug reports to capture checkout, sandbox, agent, and workflow context so issues steer agents toward the correct infrastructure surface.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug.md replaces Next.js/PostgreSQL/Browser-only prompts with harness-relevant environment fields
Bug acceptance criteria refer to regression tests or eval probes instead of app-only test assumptions
Bug acceptance criteria keep agent-browser verification conditional on deployed URL/browser behavior
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-06-17; bug template now captures checkout/branch, sandbox/container, agent runtime, command/workflow, and host context.

userStories.title: Templates reference the current git workflow rule
userStories.description: As an issue filer, I want template links and branch guidance to point at the current harness rule path and target branch convention.
acceptanceCriteria: Task, skill, audit, feature, and bug templates link to context/rules/git.md
Done/acceptance checklists mention targeting development unless the git workflow rule says otherwise
No issue template links to the stale .claude/rules/git.md path
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-06-17; all Git workflow references now use context/rules/git.md and development-target wording.

userStories.title: Template hygiene is regression-tested
userStories.description: As the harness maintainer, I want a focused test that prevents stale app-stack prompts from returning to the issue queue surface.
acceptanceCriteria: The test fails on Next.js, PostgreSQL, or a Browser-only environment prompt
The test fails on stale .claude/rules/git.md links
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-06-17; focused vitest run passes.
